title: carex acuta subsp. nigra
intro: Carex acuta subsp. nigra, also known as black-tipped sedge or black-tipped tussock sedge, is a subspecies of perennial sedge native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help alleviate digestive issues and skin problems. The plant's health benefits include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve pain, and promote wound healing. Carex acuta subsp. nigra can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically to the affected area.
